scenario: Keystone Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 with 12 Spring Boot services that issue 18000 calls per hour and still defines zeebe.client.broker.rest-address. The platform team must adopt the documented 8.8 replacement key listed in the deprecated zeebe.client.broker section before the next release.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy zeebe.client.broker.rest-address key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces zeebe.client.broker.rest-address with camunda.client.rest-address at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces zeebe.client.broker.rest-address with camunda.client.cloud.rest-address at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces zeebe.client.broker.rest-address with camunda.client.broker.rest-address at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces zeebe.client.broker.rest-address with camunda.client.grpc-address at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.

a.text: Correct. The zeebe.client.broker table maps rest-address to camunda.client.rest-address as the documented 8.8 replacement key under the unified prefix.

b.text: Incorrect. The camunda.client.cloud section does not list a rest-address entry; rest-address sits directly under camunda.client in 8.8.

c.text: Incorrect. The unified replacement drops the broker segment, so camunda.client.broker.rest-address is not in the 8.8 reference.

d.text: Incorrect. The gRPC endpoint maps to camunda.client.grpc-address; the REST address replacement is camunda.client.rest-address.

explanation: zeebe.client.broker.rest-address migrates to camunda.client.rest-address in the 8.8 reference. The unified property exposes one REST gateway endpoint for all client interaction with Camunda.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
